MRI WHOLE ABDOMEN & PELVIS
Multiecho T1 & T2 sequences in axial sagittal and coronal planes. Dynamic post-contrast vibe.
FINDINGS:
An ill defined T2 hypointense mass measuring 7.3 cm x 6.8 cm x 8.6 cm (AP X TR X CC) is seen involving the
entire cervix.
Superiorly, the mass extends into lower thirds of uterus causing severe stenosis leading to hematometra (size of
hematometra: 3.8 cm x 5 cm x 3 cm; approx vol: 40-50 mL). Laterally, bilateral parametrium is involved and
the mass is involving bilateral distal ureters causing bilateral moderate upstream hydroureteronephrosis.
Inferiorly, the upper thirds of anterior and posterior vaginal walls are involved. Anteriorly, the posterior wall of
urinary bladder is infiltrated. Posteriorly, the mass abuts the anterior wall of rectum.
Multiple enlarged necrotic bilateral external iliac, obturator lymph nodes noted, largest measuring 3 x 2 cm in
right obturator station.
Few enlarged rounded para-aortic lymph nodes, largest measuring 1.6 cm x 1 cm noted.
Bilateral pelvic walls are normal.
Liver is normal in size, shape and signal intensity with no evidence of any focal lesion. The intrahepatic biliary
radicals and common duct are not dilated.
The gall bladder is well distended and appears normal.
CBD appears normal in course and caliber.
The pancreas is normal with no evidence of any focal lesion. The peripancreatic fat planes and retroperitoneum
appear normal.
The spleen is normal in size, shape and signal intensity.
Both kidneys are normal in size, shape and outline. Pelvicalyceal systems and ureters are normal in caliber.
Both the adrenals are normal in size, shape and signal intensity pattern. No evidence of any adrenal mass lesion.
Both ovaries are normal in size and signal intensity.
No free fluid or loculated collection is seen.
Major abdominal blood vessels are normal.
Visualised bones appear grossly normal.
IMPRESSION :
Ill-defined mass measuring 7.3 cm x 6.8 cm x 8.6 cm (AP X TR X CC) involving the cervix,
leading to hematometra formation (vol: 40-5-0 cc) and invading bilateral distal ureters
causing moderate bilateral hydroureteronephrosis. Mass is also infiltrating the posterior
wall of urinary bladder.
Multiple enlarged necrotic bilateral external iliac, obturator and para-aortic lymph
nodes, suggestive of nodal involvement.
Features are suggestive of Cervical carcinoma, FIGO stage IVa.
